summary.business_type: Content/utility site — free employment-law calculators (YMYL-adjacent, pre-monetization)
top_findings: No real Core Web Vitals field data available (CrUX/GSC/Lighthouse not accessible in this environment) — lab-only technical signals are strong but should be confirmed with real PageSpeed Insights runs
Zero raster images site-wide (fully SVG/CSS) — eliminates image-weight risk but forgoes image-search visibility entirely
Sitemap lastmod dates are inconsistent in granularity — some pages stamped per-real-edit, ~150+ US state pages share a single generic 2025-01-01 verifiedYear constant

AdSense was not yet live at audit time (correctly consent + readiness gated) — ads.txt and publisher ID are in place for when it goes live; re-confirmed live via network check that adsbygoogle.js is absent from the DOM both before and after accepting the cookie banner
Excellent international SEO: complete reciprocal hreflang set (en-GB/en-US/en-CA/en-AU/fr-CA/x-default) verified live on both homepage and the fr-CA hub
Strong content depth on programmatic pages — spot-checked CA vs TX minimum-wage pages have genuinely differentiated, state-specific content, not templated filler
quick_wins: Run a real PageSpeed Insights / Lighthouse pass once the site has real traffic (CrUX needs a traffic threshold) to replace lab estimates with field data
Add an XML image sitemap only if/when image content is introduced — not needed today given zero raster images
Differentiate sitemap lastmod granularity for the ~150 US state pages currently sharing one generic verifiedYear-based date
Consider adding FAQPage schema validation via Google's Rich Results Test on a sample of calculator pages to confirm eligibility for FAQ rich snippets

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: robots.txt correctly allows all major crawlers plus explicit AI crawler allowlisting (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, CCBot, Google-Extended) — strong AI-search/GEO posture
Single canonical host: www redirects to non-www in one hop (301), http upgrades to https correctly
Canonical tags present and self-referencing correctly on every page checked
No X-Robots-Tag noindex header anywhere; meta robots explicitly 'index, follow' on calculator pages
Real 404 status (not soft-404) confirmed on unknown paths
Strong security headers: HSTS (2yr, includeSubDomains, preload), CSP, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Permissions-Policy
358 URLs in sitemap.xml, single sitemap file (no need for a sitemap index at this scale)
Complete, verified hreflang implementation (6-way reciprocal set including x-default) on both homepage and fr-CA page

findings.title: No real Core Web Vitals field data
findings.description: This audit environment cannot run Lighthouse or query CrUX/PSI APIs. Lab signals (single render-blocking stylesheet, async-loaded JS chunks, zero raster images) suggest strong LCP/CLS potential, but this is unverified against real user data.
findings.recommendation: Run PageSpeed Insights (pagespeed.web.dev) against the live URL once real traffic exists for CrUX to populate; re-audit performance category with field data.

findings.title: Sitemap lastmod granularity inconsistent across page types
findings.description: Guide/blog/FAQ pages show real per-page dateModified values (e.g. 2026-06-27). ~150+ US state pages all share a single date derived from a generic verifiedYear constant (2025-01-01) rather than genuine per-edit tracking.
findings.recommendation: Not urgent — the underlying content is genuinely state-specific and not stale, this only affects the freshness signal's precision. Consider deriving lastmod from git history or a real per-page edit timestamp if state-page content starts changing independently.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Spot-checked programmatic state pages (CA vs TX minimum wage) have genuinely different, state-specific figures and context — not templated filler with swapped placeholders
Every calculator page cites named primary/official sources (legislation.gov.uk, gov.uk, irs.gov, dol.gov, canada.ca, fairwork.gov.au style domains) directly beside the claim
Extensive same-session fact verification found and fixed several real stale/wrong figures (see Critical Issues) — remaining live figures have now been checked against primary sources
EditorialReview component correctly avoids asserting a review date it can't back up — omits the date claim entirely on pages without a real verifiedDate rather than showing vague reassurance text
Real named authorship (Jaspal Singh, Founder) consistent across About page, footer, and schema.org author/reviewedBy fields — not a fabricated 'editorial team'

findings.title: No independent readability/reading-level scoring tool available in this environment
findings.description: Could not run a Flesch-Kincaid or similar automated readability pass. Manual spot-reads of calculator FAQ copy suggest plain-English, appropriately scoped for a general consumer audience.
findings.recommendation: Low priority — content reads clearly in manual review. Revisit if a readability tool becomes available.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Single H1 per page, logical H2 hierarchy observed on every page sampled (redundancy calculator, guides, state pages)
Title tags and meta descriptions are unique and specific per page, not templated boilerplate (verified across calculator, state, and guide page types)
Breadcrumb navigation present and marked up on tool pages

Internal linking is dense and relevant — Related tools/guides sections, footer category links, mega-menu, all cross-link real, live pages (verified extensively this session — several previously-broken internal links were found and fixed)

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: Multiple JSON-LD blocks per page (BreadcrumbList, WebApplication, FAQPage confirmed present via source review)
Article/Guide schema includes both author (Person, real named founder) and reviewedBy (Organization) fields
WebApplication schema includes isAccessibleForFree: true and an offers block with correct currency-by-region logic

findings.title: Schema validity not run through Google's Rich Results Test in this session
findings.description: JSON-LD structure was reviewed in source and confirmed present on rendered pages, but not validated against Google's live Rich Results Test tool, which can catch subtle type/property errors static review misses.
findings.recommendation: Spot-check 3-5 representative page types (calculator, guide, FAQ, blog, state page) through search.google.com/test/rich-results before the next major schema change.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Zero raster images site-wide — no LCP risk from unoptimized images, no CLS risk from image loading
Single render-blocking stylesheet in <head>; all JS chunks load async
Static export (Next.js output: 'export') served from Cloudflare's edge — no server-side render latency

findings.title: No field data (CrUX) or lab data (Lighthouse) available to score this category directly
findings.description: Score above is inferred from structural/lab-adjacent signals only (resource loading pattern, response times observed via curl: 0.49s-1.82s across 3 samples, likely reflecting CDN cache state rather than app performance).
findings.recommendation: Run a real PageSpeed Insights test post-launch/at-traffic to replace this inferred score with real LCP/INP/CLS numbers.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: robots.txt explicitly allows GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, CCBot, and Google-Extended — deliberate GEO/AI-citation posture, not accidental
Every calculator page structures content as: direct answer/estimate, named source, FAQ Q&A pairs — highly extractable format for AI Overviews/answer engines
FAQPage schema present, giving AI crawlers structured Q&A pairs directly
Source-adjacent citation pattern (source link placed immediately next to the specific claim it supports) mirrors what AI answer engines look for when selecting a citable passage

findings.title: No llms.txt file
findings.description: llms.txt is an emerging, not-yet-standardized convention some sites use to guide AI crawlers to canonical content. Not having one is not a real deficiency today.
findings.recommendation: Optional. Low priority — monitor adoption before investing here.

categories.name: Images
what_works: Zero raster tags found across every page sampled (homepage, blog, guides, about) — site is built entirely with inline SVG and CSS
No missing-alt-text risk since there are no raster images to caption
OG/Twitter card images are dynamically generated (Next.js opengraph-image route) at correct 1200x630 dimensions with alt text set

findings.title: No opportunity for image-search traffic
findings.description: The all-SVG approach is a deliberate, reasonable performance tradeoff, but it means the site captures zero Google Images search traffic, which can be a meaningful channel for some illustrative/explainer content.
findings.recommendation: Not a fix — a product decision. Worth revisiting only if image-search traffic becomes a stated growth goal.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: None outstanding — the critical-severity issues found across this session's broader audit work (stale tribunal compensation cap, stale Vento bands, wrong Class 4 NI rate, dead source links, AdSense consent-gate regression) have already been found and fixed in prior commits this session.

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Run a real PageSpeed Insights / Lighthouse audit once traffic exists to replace lab-inferred performance scores with field data
Validate JSON-LD on 3-5 representative page types via Google's Rich Results Test

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Consider deriving US state page lastmod dates from real per-edit history rather than the shared generic verifiedYear constant
Continue the calculator-by-calculator source verification sweep for the ~15 calculators not yet individually re-checked against live official sources this session

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Once AdSense goes fully live, re-verify the consent gate is still enforced after any future refactor (this was found broken and fixed once already this session)
Re-run this audit quarterly, or after any major template/schema change

artifacts.notes: Desktop (1280px) and mobile (375px) above-the-fold screenshots of the homepage and a calculator page were captured and reviewed for this pass but are not persisted as PNG files -- this environment has no binary file-write path from the screenshot tool to disk. See findings/visual.md for the written findings from that review.
